title: NQ Measured Move Ladder Context
focus_questions: Is the market still carrying the same real leg?
What threshold was actually body-confirmed before the target was upgraded?

summary.thesis: Measured move works best as a ladder of body-confirmed thresholds inside one continuous leg, not as a random wick-based projection.
summary.operator_translation: Measure the real leg, not the noise, and do not upgrade targets before confirmation.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card to filter measured-move claims and keep only the ones supported by leg continuity and body confirmation.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: an active continuous leg with measurable threshold progress. Required evidence: one real leg, body-confirmed threshold clears, and continuity of the campaign. Do not trade: if the market is choppy, overlapping, or only printing wick noise. Real trigger: confirmed progression through the next ladder stage. Failure point: the next threshold never confirms and the leg loses continuity.

when_relevant: The market is still carrying one real impulsive leg.
Target decisions depend on whether the next threshold is truly confirmed.
The operator needs to know whether to escalate or reduce the target ladder.
required_evidence: the leg being measured is continuous enough to count as one campaign
threshold clears are body-confirmed instead of wick-only
the move can still be tracked through distinct ladder stages
confirmation_signals: the leg remains continuous while price progresses through thresholds
body closes confirm the next stage before the target ladder is upgraded
reaction near each threshold makes it clear whether expansion is alive or stalling
invalidation_signals: the leg breaks into overlapping chop and loses continuity
the market shifts into a different script before target escalation is justified
no_trade_conditions: Do not measure random wicks, overlapping chop, or unrelated fragments as one clean leg.
Do not upgrade the target ladder before body confirmation appears.
Do not force measured-move logic when the broader script has already shifted into balance or reversal.

entry_archetypes.name: confirmed threshold continuation
entry_archetypes.description: Stay with the move only after the next threshold is body-confirmed.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the confirmation is only a wick touch without close quality.

entry_archetypes.name: failed threshold downgrade
entry_archetypes.description: Reduce ambition or flatten when the next ladder stage fails to confirm.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid inventing a larger target after failed confirmation.

management_notes: Keep the active ladder stage explicit in review.
If the next threshold fails to confirm, downgrade the script instead of forcing projection.
Measured move should support context and management, not replace location logic.
review_questions: Was the operator measuring the real leg or just reacting to screen noise?
What body-confirmed threshold actually justified target escalation?
Did the market keep the same leg alive, or had it already transitioned into a different script?
